namespace DirectSumDecomposition

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D₁, D₂` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D₁` is a refinement of `D₂` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D₂`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D₁` such that `∑ T ∈ B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D₁ D₂ : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃N⦄, N ∈ D₂ → ∃ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ⊆ D₁ ∧ N = sSup B

/-- The direct sum decomposition `refinement B hB hB' hB''` is a refinement of `D` -/

import Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Module
import PersistentDecomp.Mathlib.Order.Disjoint
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.Indecomposable
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.
-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ⊤
  not_bot_mem' : ⊥ ∉ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D₁ := by cases D₁; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) :=
  D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ⊥ ∉ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ↦ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor <;> sorry
  --rw [← iSup_apply, ← sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D₁, D₂` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D₁` is a refinement of `D₂` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D₂`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D₁` such that `∑ T ∈ B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D₁ D₂ : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃N⦄, N ∈ D₂ → ∃ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ⊆ D₁ ∧ N = sSup B

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ≤ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, ∃ A : I, N.val ≤ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ⟨N₀, h_not_le⟩
  have h_ge : N₀.val ⊔ sSup I ≤ sSup J := by
    rw [← sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ⟨h_n⟩
    · exact (le_sSup (h_n ▸ N₀.prop))
    · rename b ∈ {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ≤ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ▸ h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ∃ A : I, C ≤ A.val ∧ C ∈ J}
  have h_sub : B ⊆ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [B] at h_C_mem
    exact h_C_mem.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    · intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ⟨h₁, _⟩
      rcases h₁ with ⟨a, h_a, h_le⟩
      exact (le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le)
    have h_le_subset (A : I) : ∃ C ⊆ B, A ≤ sSup C := by
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ⟨f A.prop, ?_, by rw [← (hf' A.prop)]⟩
      intro α h_α
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ⟨⟨A, A.prop, ?_⟩, hf A.prop h_α⟩
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_α
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ⟨A, h_A_mem⟩ with ⟨C, h_C⟩
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N₀.val ∉ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra

import Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Module
import PersistentDecomp.Mathlib.Order.Disjoint
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.Indecomposable
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.
-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decompositon of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ⊤
  not_bot_mem' : ⊥ ∉ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D₁ := by cases D₁; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) :=
  D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ⊥ ∉ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ↦ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor <;> sorry
  --rw [← iSup_apply, ← sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D₁, D₂` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `D₁` is a refinement of `D₂` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D₁` such that `∑ T ∈ B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D₁ D₂ : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃N⦄, N ∈ D₂ → ∃ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ⊆ D₁ ∧ N = sSup B

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ≤ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, ∃ A : I, N.val ≤ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ⟨N₀, h_not_le⟩
  have h_ge : N₀.val ⊔ sSup I ≤ sSup J := by
    rw [← sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ⟨h_n⟩
    · exact (le_sSup (h_n ▸ N₀.prop))
    · rename b ∈ {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ≤ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ▸ h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ∃ A : I, C ≤ A.val ∧ C ∈ J}
  have h_sub : B ⊆ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with ⟨A, h_A, h_C⟩
    exact h_C
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    · intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ⟨A, h_A, h_le⟩
      rcases h_A with ⟨a, h_a, h_le'⟩
      exact (le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le')
    have h_le_subset (A : I) : ∃ C ⊆ B, A ≤ sSup C := by
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ⟨f A.prop, ?_, by rw [← (hf' A.prop)]⟩
      intro α h_α
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ⟨⟨A, A.prop, ?_⟩, hf A.prop h_α⟩
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_α

    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)

    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ⟨A, h₁, h₂⟩
    exact (h_not_le (⟨A, h₁⟩) h₂)

    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N₀.prop h_sub h_aux')

    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ▸ N₀.prop)

    rw [← h_aux]

    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (⊤ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ⊤ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge

/-- The refinement relation defines a preorder on direct sum decompositions of `M` via `D₁ ≤ D₂` if
and only if `D₂` is a refinement of `D₁` i.e. this relation is reflexive and transitive. -/
instance : Preorder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where

        have h_last : sSup B = (⊥ : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [← h_B₁] at h_last
        subst h_last
        exact (J.not_bot_mem h_N_in_J)
      have h_A_le_N : A.val ≤ N := by
        rw [h_B₁]
        exact le_sSup h_mem

        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)

        exact h_A_eq_N ▸ A.prop

      sorry
    aesop

    sorry

    (hB : ∀ N hN, N = sSup (B N hN))
    (hB' : ∀ N hN, sSupIndep (B N hN))

We need to prove the theorem `refinement_le`. Let's think about how to construct this proof.

First, we need to understand what the refined decomposition is. The refined decomposition is the union of all the submodules in the sets `B N hN` for `N` in `D`. So we need to show that every submodule in `D` is also in this union.

Let's think about how to show this. For any submodule `A` in `D`, we need to show that `A` is in the union. The union is constructed by taking, for each `N` in `D`, a set of submodules of `N` (which are also submodules of `M`) and collecting them all.

So, for the specific submodule `A` in `D`, we know that `A` is a submodule of `M` and `A ≠ 0`. We need to show that `A` appears in the union. Since `A` is in `D`, we can apply the given properties.

For the specific `N = A` in `D`, we have:
- `hB A (A.prop) : A = sSup (B A (A.prop))`
- `hB' A (A.prop) : sSupIndep (B A (A.prop))`
- `hB'' A (A.prop) : ⊥ ∉ B A (A.prop)`

The set `B A (A.prop)` is a set of submodules of `A` (which are also submodules of `M`). The supremum of these submodules is `A`. Since `A` is in `D`, it's non-zero, and the set `B A (A.prop)` is non-empty (it contains at least `A` itself, because `sSup (B A (A.prop)) = A` and `A ≠ 0`).

Now, to show that `A` is in the union. The union is `⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN`. So for `N = A` and `hN = A.prop`, we have the set `B A (A.prop)`. The union contains `B A (A.prop)`, and `A` is in `B A (A.prop)` because `sSup (B A (A.prop)) = A`.

Wait, actually, `A` is the supremum of `B A (A.prop)`, but `A` itself may not be in `B A (A.prop)`. Let's think more carefully.

The set `B A (A.prop)` is a set of submodules of `A`. The supremum of these submodules is `A`. This means that `A` is the least upper bound for the set `B A (A.prop)`. It doesn't necessarily mean that `A` is in the set `B A (A.prop)`.

However, we know from `hB''` that `⊥ ∉ B A (A.prop)`. Since `A` is non-zero (from `D.not_bot_mem'`), and `A` is the supremum of `B A (A.prop)`, if `A` were not in `B A (A.prop)`, then `sSup (B A (A.prop))` would be a proper submodule of `A`, which would mean `A` is not the supremum. This is a contradiction.

Wait, let's think more carefully. Suppose `A` is not in `B A (A.prop)`. Then `sSup (B A (A.prop))` is the least upper bound of the set `B A (A.prop)`. Since `A` is the supremum of `B A (A.prop)` by `hB`, and `A` is not in `B A (A.prop)`, then `sSup (B A (A.prop))` must be a submodule strictly containing `A` or equal to `A`.

Wait, let's use the property of `sSup`. We know that `A = sSup (B A (A.prop))`. The definition of `sSup` is the least upper bound. So `A` is the least upper bound of `B A (A.prop)`. If `A` is not in `B A (A.prop)`, then `sSup (B A (A.prop))` must be a submodule that is an upper bound for all submodules in `B A (A.prop)` and is minimal with that property. But `A` is already an upper bound, so `sSup (B A (A.prop))` must be equal to `A`.

This reasoning shows that `A` must be in `B A (A.prop)`. Let's see how to formalize this in Lean.

We need to show that for any `A ∈ D`, `A` is in the union `⋃ N, ⋃ hN, B N hN`. We can take `N = A` and `hN = A.prop`. Then we need to show that `A` is in `B A (A.prop)`. 

From `hB A (A.prop)`, we have `A = sSup (B A (A.prop))`. From `hB'' A (A.prop)`, we have `⊥ ∉ B A (A.prop)`. From `D.not_bot_mem'`, we have `A ≠ ⊥`.

Now, if `A` were not in `B A (A.prop)`, then `sSup (B A (A.prop))` would be a submodule different from `A`. But we know `sSup (B A (A.prop)) = A`. This creates a contradiction.

open CategoryTheory
open DirectSum
open CompleteLattice

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K}

  coe := carrier

  D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'

  ∀ ⦃N⦄, N ∈ D₂ → ∃ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ⊆ D₁ ∧ N = sSup B

  have h_ge : N₀.val ⊔ sSup I ≤ sSup J := by
    rw [← sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem

    · exact (le_sSup (h_n ▸ N₀.prop))
    · rename b ∈ {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i

        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i

    rcases h_C_mem with ⟨h₁, _⟩

import Mathlib.Algebra.DirectSum.Module
import PersistentDecomp.Mathlib.Order.Disjoint
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.Indecomposable
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.
-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ⊤
  not_bot_mem' : ⊥ ∉ carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' D₁ := by cases D₁; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) :=
  D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ⊥ ∉ D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : ⨆ p ∈ D, p x = ⊤ := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I ↦ p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor <;> sorry
  --rw [← iSup_apply, ← sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `D₁, D₂` be direct sum decompositions of `M` we say that `D₁` is a refinement of `D₂` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `D₁` such that `∑ T ∈ B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (D₁ D₂ : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃N⦄, N ∈ D₂ → ∃ B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ⊆ D₁ ∧ N = sSup B

variable {M : C ⥤ ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N ≤ A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ∀ N : J, ∃ A : I, N.val ≤ A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with ⟨N₀, h_not_le⟩
  have h_ge : N₀.val ⊔ sSup I ≤ sSup J := by
    rw [← sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with ⟨h_n⟩
    · exact (le_sSup (h_n ▸ N₀.prop))
    · rename b ∈ {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) ≤ sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i ▸ h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | ∃ A : I, C ≤ A.val ∧ C ∈ J}
  have h_sub : B ⊆ J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with ⟨A, _, h_C⟩
    exact h_C
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    · intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with ⟨A, h_A, h_le⟩
      exact (le_sSup_of_le h_A h_le)
    have h_le_subset (A : I) : ∃ C ⊆ B, A ≤ sSup C := by
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine ⟨f A.prop, ?_, by rw [← (hf' A.prop)]⟩
      intro α h_α
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine ⟨⟨A, A.prop, ?_⟩, hf A.prop h_α⟩
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_α
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset ⟨A, h_A_mem⟩ with ⟨C, h_C, h_le⟩
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : N₀.val ∉ B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with ⟨A, h₁, h₂⟩
    exact (h_not_le (⟨A, h₁⟩) h₂)
  have h_disj : Disjoint N₀.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' N₀.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : N₀.val ≠ ⊥ := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra ▸ N₀.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < N₀.val ⊔ sSup I := by
    rw [← h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (⊤ : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ⊤ := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (⊤ : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra
